metadata.title: DARTFormer: Finding The Best Type Of Attention
organizations: University of Cambridge, Imperial College London, University of Oxford

sections.title: Motivation
content: There is a wide range of efficient Transformer attention mechanisms whose relative performance often depends on the task
Different attention heads are supposed to pick up different patterns
DARTS-like neural architecture search is highly effective at finding good network topologies

sections.title: Attention Search
content: Idea: Can we apply a DARTS-like method to identify good and bad attention mechanisms?
By training a supernetwork with heads that have different attention types in each layer, we can then use masked validation to identify the current best and worse attention type for that layer.

figures.description: Diagram of the DARTFormer attention search supernetwork showing multiple heads with different attention types being aggregated to an output.

sections.title: Optimal Homogeneous Attention
content: Applying the attention search idea to a single layer Transformer and picking the best performing attention, we can hope to identify the best attention for that task when operating in a full Transformer model.

figures.description: Flowchart showing the search process for Bigbird and Synthesizer attention types.
Flowchart showing the search process for Performer attention types across multiple layers.

sections.title: Optimal Heterogenous Attention
content: Method 1: Attention search on a single layer pruning the worst heads until we have the right size for a single layer. Stack this layer and retrain from scratch.
Method 2: Attention search on a single layer and then take the top 4 scoring mechanisms. Stack a uniform mix and retrain from scratch.
Method 3: Attention search across many layers, picking the best attention in each one. Retrain from scratch with possible different attention in each layer

sections.title: Experimental Results
content: Heterogeneous attention fails to outperform the best homogeneous models

tables.description: Table comparing accuracy and scores of various attention types (Bigbird, Linformer, Performer, etc.) across Text Classification, Listops, and Document Matching tasks.
Table comparing the best homogeneous models against proposed Methods 1, 2, and 3 across different classification and matching tasks.

sections.title: Conclusion
content: A DARTS-like method can often be used to find the best attention for a task by training only a single layer
Searching for and using heterogeneous 'mixed attention' Transformers does not give good results.
Perhaps different mechanisms do not provide as large functional biases, or the biases are less useful for learning good representations in the attention layer than was previously thought
